key_insight: Anharmonic calculations reveal that RSS CoCrNi stacking faults remain unstable with increasing temperature, contrary to previous harmonic predictions.
engineering_relevance: This research challenges existing models and provides insights essential for the development of high-performance materials in extreme conditions.
method: Used a near-quantum-accuracy machine learning interatomic potential.
Employed the fully anharmonic projected average force integrator for calculations.
Conducted molecular dynamics simulations to assess dislocation behavior.
evidence_signal: Anharmonic calculations demonstrated that the RSS intrinsic stacking-fault energy decreases with temperature, maintaining a negative value.
watch_item: The reliance on machine learning potentials may introduce uncertainty in extrapolating results to real-world conditions.
paper_title: Finite Temperature Stacking Fault Stability in Random and Locally Ordered CoCrNi beyond the Harmonic Approximation
